portfolio_structure.design: 9 positions across 3 categories × 3 risk profiles. Optimized for risk-adjusted EV given user preference (ADM > NTR > WEAT > IPI > BG > CORN > MOS). MOS dropped per 'optimize'.

atm_long.label: Long-dated ATM
atm_long.desc: Core upside, lower risk. Long-dated, near-the-money. Captures both spot + IV expansion.

mid_otm.label: Mid-tenor OTM
mid_otm.desc: Balanced risk/reward. Mid tenor (4-8 mo), modestly OTM.

near_otm.label: Near-term OTM
near_otm.desc: Maximum convexity. Short-dated, deep OTM. Pays multiples in bull, zero in bear.

positions.thesis: Core wheat catch-up bet. ATM Jan '27 LEAP on the futures-tracking ETF — captures both the underlying repricing and IV expansion.
Mid-tenor balanced bet. 9% OTM at 165 DTE — captures the harvest-pricing window if the move lands by October.
Cheapest convexity bet in the entire portfolio. 16% OTM at 109 DTE for $0.48 — if corn rallies on yield-loss math, this prints 5-15× cost.
Largest fertilizer position. World's largest potash + nitrogen producer, ATM Jan '27 LEAP. Captures the full North American crop year.
High-beta small-cap potash with mid-tenor runway. 12% OTM at 228 DTE. 71% IV means even a moderate IPI move prints multiples.
Maximum convexity. 16% OTM at 74 DTE for $1.10 — pure asymmetric bet on near-term nitrogen squeeze. Zero in base/bear, prints 8-15× in bull.
Anchor of the portfolio. ADM is user's #1 conviction name and the most-diversified business. Mar '27 ATM LEAP gives 10+ months for full transmission.
Bunge OTM LEAP for ag-equity diversification. Largest grain merchandiser; freight surcharges + wheat carry both kick in. 7% OTM at 256 DTE.
Maximum convexity on the highest-conviction name. 7% OTM at 137 DTE — keeps ADM dominance and offers 4-8× upside if ADM trades back to 2008 oil-shock peak P/E.

_meta.note: User-stated probabilities sum to 105%. Internally normalized to 100% for EV computation. Each scenario revalues all 15 options independently via Black-Scholes with IV expansion (+20% bull, -15% bear, neutral base).

superBear.label: Super Bear: Hormuz opens tomorrow
superBear.narrative: Miracle scenario. Hormuz opens overnight, all trapped tankers exit within 2 weeks. Trump subsidizes farmers AND lifts ag import sanctions. China simultaneously announces a 25-30 MMT wheat export release. Crop yields suffer 5-10% from missed fertilizer windows but the subsidy + supply flood overwhelms the catch-up. Net: ~-5% from current spot after volatility.

taco.label: Untimely TACO: Trump folds, ships clear too late

yield_impact.wheat_impact: minimal — HRW already topdressed in March-April

taco.narrative: Trump pulls the US blockade in 2 weeks, signs an Iran deal cosmetically '1% better' than Obama's. Tankers exit Hormuz over 21-28 days. Persian Gulf → US Gulf transit is 30 days. Plus 7-14 days port/rail/truck distribution. Fertilizer at farms early-mid July — pre-plant and V4-V6 sidedress windows already missed. Only late V8-V12 sidedress possible, causing 10-15% N-related yield loss (5-8% net after farmer pre-positioning). Inelastic demand drives corn +17-22%, wheat +10-15%.

tacoChina.label: TACO + China dump
tacoChina.narrative: Same logistics as 2a, but China simultaneously announces a 25 MMT wheat export release. Wheat catch-up fully offset by Chinese supply. Corn unaffected by China (China is a net corn importer). Fertilizer equities flat — supply normalizes faster than equity rerating.

base.label: Base: Oil to $130 by end of May
base.narrative: Oil steadily rises to $140-150/bbl by end of May. Stock market sells off, bond market panics, Trump capitulates and takes a previously-rejected Iran deal. Use $130/bbl as the relative analog (averaging the spike + retracement). Apply 0.55-0.65× transmission ratio — wheat +55%, corn +40%, fertilizer +35-45%.

bull.label: Bull: Hormuz closed through June, $200/bbl
bull.narrative: Hormuz remains closed through June. WTI hits $200/bbl. Demand destruction begins to bite but doesn't fully clear the supply gap. Energy + fertilizer second-order shock fully transmits to grain. Wheat could revisit 2008 ($440/MT) or 2022 ($520/MT) peak levels.

bullPlus.label: Bull++: True clearing price · $300/bbl
bullPlus.narrative: Hormuz extended closure. The 9 Mbpd Hormuz shortfall exceeds non-OPEC spare capacity. FGE/Bloomberg stress-test: only price clears the market via demand destruction. Oil triples to $250-300/bbl. Soft commodities reprice proportionally with full-strength 1.0×+ transmission on a 200%-from-spot oil shock.

china_stockpile_context.source: USDA WASDE Apr 2026 + FAS PSD Mar 2026 China report
